subject: [RFC PATCH 29/45] mm: page_alloc: refuse fragmenting fallback for callers with cheap fallback

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch modifies the page allocation mechanism to refuse fragmentation fallbacks for certain callers that have a cheap fallback option. It introduces a new flag, ALLOC_HIGHORDER_OPTIONAL, which is set for callers with explicit fallback declarations or atomic-context shapes. The patch also adds a struct to record tainted SPB walks and checks for tainted SPBs in the __rmqueue function. If a tainted SPB has free pages or a free pageblock that could absorb an allocation after evacuation, the patch returns NULL and bumps SPB_HIGHORDER_REFUSED. This prevents fragmentation fallbacks for callers with cheap fallback options, allowing truly must-not-fail consumers to proceed with their allocations.
